question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category

question: List the top 5 customers by the number of orders placed and their total spent amount.
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_spent FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 5

question: Which products have been ordered the most and what is the total quantity sold for each?
sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_quantity DESC
notes: Multiple joins with aggregation and ordering

question: Which products have been sold the most in terms of quantity, along with their total sales revenue?
sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) as total_quantity, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) as total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_quantity DESC
notes: Aggregates quantity and revenue per product

question: Which products have been sold the most, and what is the total revenue generated from them?
sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Aggregates sales data and sorts by revenue

question: Identify the top 5 customers by total spending in the last year, along with their email and country.
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, c.email, c.country, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_spent FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.order_date >= DATE '2022-10-01' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name, c.email, c.country ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 5
notes: Involves filtering by date, multiple JOINs, and ordering by total spending

question: List the customers who have not made any purchases in the last year along with their signup date.
sql: SELECT c.name, c.signup_date FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id AND o.order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year' WHERE o.id IS NULL
notes: Using LEFT JOIN to find customers without orders

question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category;

question: Which customers have not made any orders in the last 6 months and their signup dates?
sql: SELECT c.name, c.signup_date FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id AND o.order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '6 month' WHERE o.id IS NULL
notes: Uses LEFT JOIN to find customers without recent orders

question: What is the total amount spent by customers from Canada on completed orders in 2023?
sql: SELECT SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_spent FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE c.country = 'Canada' AND o.status = 'completed' AND o.order_date BETWEEN '2023-01-01' AND '2023-12-31'
notes: JOIN across 3 tables with date filtering

question: How many completed orders were placed by customers from each country in the last year?
sql: SELECT c.country, COUNT(o.id) AS completed_orders FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.order_date >= DATE '2022-01-01' GROUP BY c.country
notes: JOIN across 2 tables with filtering and aggregation

question: Which customers have not made any purchases in the last year?
sql: SELECT c.name, c.email FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id AND o.order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year' WHERE o.id IS NULL;

question: Which products have been ordered the most and what is the total revenue generated from those products?
sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_quantity DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Multiple JOINs with aggregation and filtering

question: Which products have the highest total revenue generated from completed orders in the last quarter?
sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.order_date >= DATE '2023-07-01' GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
notes: JOIN across 3 tables with filtering, aggregation, and sorting

question: Which products have not been sold in the last 6 months?
sql: SELECT p.id, p.name FROM products p LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id LEFT JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id AND o.status = 'completed' WHERE o.order_date < DATE '2023-04-01' OR o.id IS NULL
notes: Uses LEFT JOIN to find unsold products with date filtering

question: Find the average quantity of products ordered per order for completed orders.
sql: SELECT AVG(total_quantity) AS avg_quantity_per_order FROM (SELECT o.id, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY o.id) AS order_totals
